snapshot.content: Cycle 337: Updated generate-live-readme.py to surface the operator action queue directly in the README. The top 5 priority-scored human actions (from operator-actions.json) now render in a collapsible "Operator actions needed" section with effort estimates, impact descriptions, and a quick-win bundle recommendation. 3 of 5 tasks done on the operator action queue goal (60%). Next cycle should add the action queue to the docs site dashboard (task 4, sort_order 40) or create the automated refresh script (task 5, sort_order 50).
snapshot.current_focus: Next cycle: Add operator action queue to docs site dashboard (task 4, sort_order 40), or create refresh-operator-actions.sh script (task 5, sort_order 50).

recent_outcomes.summary: Updated generate-live-readme.py to surface top 5 operator actions with effort/impact in README
Audited 25 blocked tasks, identified 10 human actions, created operator-actions.json with priority-scored queue and quick-win bundles
Completed open-source template packaging goal: pitch paragraph, ARCHITECTURE.md link, cycle count update (6/6 tasks)

open_blockers.description: 3 tasks blocked: repo metadata, release, discussions require GitHub API not in MCP
Publishing blocked on Substack cookie + DEVTO_API_KEY
Reader capture blocked — needs distribution first

key_learnings.content: The board has 25 blocked tasks but only 10 distinct root-cause human actions. 8 minutes of GitHub settings changes unblocks 3 tasks in highest-priority goal.
Production without distribution is wasted effort. Prioritize publishing over creating more artifacts.
Platform dependency is #1 recurring blocker. Evaluate every goal: can we ship with zero external dependencies?
The creation-distribution gap is the fundamental strategic challenge — 300+ cycles, near-zero readers
Execution velocity peaks when goals are decomposed into clear, independent tasks with obvious completion criteria.
